title: Pixie PxL Script
description: Schema representing a Pixie PxL script definition including its source code, arguments, and execution metadata. PxL is a Python-dialect domain-specific language for querying eBPF-collected telemetry data in Kubernetes clusters.

id.description: Unique identifier of the PxL script.

name.description: Human-readable name of the PxL script.

description.description: Description of what the script does and what telemetry data it queries.

pxl.description: Source code of the PxL script. Must be valid PxL, a Python-dialect DSL. Scripts typically import the px module, create DataFrames from Pixie tables, apply filters and aggregations, and call px.display() to output results.

vis.description: Optional visualization specification describing how to render the script's output in the Pixie UI.

args.description: List of argument definitions for parameterizing the script.

hidden.description: If true, the script is hidden from the Pixie Live UI script selector.

orgID.description: Identifier of the organization that owns this script.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the script was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the script was last updated.

ScriptArg.description: An argument definition for a parameterized PxL script, enabling scripts to accept runtime input values.

name.description: Name of the argument, matching the parameter name in the PxL script function signature.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this argument controls.

defaultValue.description: Default value for this argument when not provided by the caller.

validValues.description: Optional list of valid values for this argument, used to populate dropdowns in the UI.

semantic_type.description: Semantic type hint for argument rendering in the Pixie Live UI.

VisSpec.description: Visualization specification defining how to render a PxL script's output in the Pixie Live UI.

variables.description: Variable definitions for the visualization, bound to script arguments.

widgets.description: Widget definitions describing how each output table should be visualized.

globalFuncOutputName.description: Name of the global function to call when the visualization renders.

VisVariable.description: A variable in the visualization spec, binding a UI control to a PxL script argument.

name.description: Name of the variable, matching a PxL script argument name.

type.description: Type of the variable controlling which UI widget is rendered.

description.description: Description displayed in the UI for this variable.

VisWidget.description: A visualization widget displaying PxL script output in the Pixie Live UI.

name.description: Name of the widget, used as a label in the UI.

WidgetPosition.description: Position and dimensions of a widget in the dashboard grid layout.

WidgetFunc.description: Reference to a PxL function that produces data for this widget.

name.description: Name of the PxL function to call.

args.description: Arguments to pass to the PxL function, bound to visualization variables.

name.description: Argument name matching the PxL function parameter.

variable.description: Name of the visualization variable to bind to this argument.

DisplaySpec.description: Display configuration for a visualization widget specifying chart type and axes.

@type.description: Protobuf type URL identifying the display specification type.

title.description: Chart title displayed in the UI.

examples.name: HTTP Overview
examples.description: Shows an overview of HTTP traffic in the cluster including request rates, latency, and error rates.
examples.pxl:
import px
df = px.DataFrame(table='http_events', start_time='-5m')
df = df[['time_', 'upid', 'req_path', 'req_method', 'resp_status', 'resp_latency_ns']]
px.display(df, 'http_events')

args.name: start_time
args.description: Start time for the query window.
